perturbation.original_text: This Mutual Non-Disclosur e Agreemen t (“Agreement” ) is made and entere d into on the date signe d below by and between _________________________________ (hereinafter “COMPANY”) and Dealer Pay, LLC (hereinafter “Dealer Pay” ).
perturbation.changed_text: This Mutual Non-Disclosur e Agreemen t (“Agreement” ) is made and entere d into on the date signe d below by and between _________________________________ (hereinafter “Partner”) and Dealer Pay, LLC (hereinafter “Dealer Pay” ).
perturbation.explanation: The term 'COMPANY' is replaced with 'Partner.' Throughout the document, 'COMPANY' is used to define obligations and rights. Changing this to 'Partner' introduces uncertainty as to whether 'COMPANY' and 'Partner' are the same entity, particularly where 'COMPANY' is used in subsequent clauses outlining specific duties.
perturbation.contradicted_text: WHEREAS , Deale r Pay and CO MPAN Y desire to e xplor e the possibilit y of enterin g into one or more potential busine ss transaction s or relationship s (each a “Busine ss Relationship”) , with the understandin g that any such Busine ss Relationsh ip wo uld be embodie d in a m utuall y acceptable , definitiv e written agreemen t execute d by the parties ; and WHEREAS , in connectio n with any Busine ss Relationsh ip and any discussions , demonstrations , evaluation s and negotiation s concernin g a potentia l Busine ss Relationsh ip (“Discussions”) , each party and/o r its affiliate s and/or their respectiv e Representative s (as suc h term is d efine d below) , may receive , observ e and/o r have physica l or electroni c acce ss to certain Co nfidentia l Informatio n (as define d below) of the other party and/o r its affiliates ; and WHEREAS , Deale r Pay and COMPAN Y desire to e nsure tha t appropria te confidentialit y obligation s are in pla ce to prote ct Confidentia l Informatio n from unauthorize d access , use and disclosure.
perturbation.justification: The original text defines one party as 'COMPANY', and the contradicted text relies on this definition, referencing 'COMPANY' throughout. The changed text replaces 'COMPANY' with 'Partner'. This creates a contradiction because the contradicted text refers to 'COMPANY', but the changed text now refers to 'Partner'. This misalignment of terminology, as defined in the Perturbation Definitions, introduces uncertainty and a direct conflict, making it unclear whether the obligations and rights outlined for 'COMPANY' apply to 'Partner', creating a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: A.“Confidential Information” as used herein any and all confidential and/or proprietary information concerning the Originating Party’s business and such party’s trade secrets, proprietary data and business data, whether oral or written, tangible or intangible, which is disclosed to or learned by the Receiving Party in the course of the Discussions or otherwise while working with the Originating Party, and/or discovered, developed, conceived, originat ed, appreciably modified, or prepared in scope of Receiving Party’s relationship with the Originating Party, including but not limited to the following:
perturbation.changed_text: A.“Confidential Information” as used herein means only written documents explicitly marked as confidential concerning the Originating Party’s business and such party’s trade secrets, proprietary data and business data which is disclosed to or learned by the Receiving Party in the course of formal meetings and subsequently approved in writing by the Originating Party's legal counsel.
perturbation.explanation: The definition of 'Confidential Information' is significantly narrowed to only include written documents explicitly marked confidential, learned only during formal meetings, and then approved by legal counsel. This directly contradicts the later sections, which discusses other types of confidential information.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 11.Product development strategies, expansion plans, and internal business plans, procedures, or methods of operation; and
perturbation.justification: The changed text significantly narrows the definition of 'Confidential Information' to only written documents explicitly marked as confidential, learned during formal meetings, and approved by legal counsel. This directly contradicts section 11 which includes 'Product development strategies, expansion plans, and internal business plans, procedures, or methods of operation' as confidential information, which are not necessarily written or approved by legal counsel. The changed text thus creates a direct conflict with the contradicted text because the latter can be construed as other types of confidential information besides just written documentation.

perturbation.original_text: D.“Receiving Party” as used herein shall refer to the party who receives the Confidential Information or Intellectual Property from the Originating Party.
perturbation.changed_text: D.“Receiving Party” as used herein shall refer to the party who actively solicits and obtains the Confidential Information or Intellectual Property from the Originating Party with explicit written consent.
perturbation.explanation: The definition of 'Receiving Party' is modified to include the conditions that it actively solicits and receives Confidential Information with explicit written consent. This creates a contradiction with instances where the Receiving Party might passively receive information or not solicit it at all.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Both parties recognizes and acknowledges that, in an effort to foster the Discussions one or both of the pa rties has provided and/or will provide, at no cost, fee, charge or expense to Receiving Party, Confidential Information of a special and unique value and nature developed and/or acquired by (and/or being developed or acquired by) Originating Party at great expense and cost to Originating Party which, if it were to come into the possession of Originating Party’s competitors, would cause irreparable damage to Originating Party, its competitive advantage and its ability to operate profitably.
perturbation.justification: The original text defines the 'Receiving Party' as simply the party that receives confidential information. The changed text adds the condition that the Receiving Party must actively solicit and obtain the information with explicit written consent. This contradicts the provided 'Contradicted Text' which acknowledges that information 'has provided and/or will provide, at no cost, fee, charge or expense to Receiving Party' indicating that the Receiving Party could passively receive information without solicitation or explicit consent. Therefore, the modified definition creates an in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: V. NON -SOLICITATION OF EMPLOYEES AND/OR CONTRACTORS: Receiving Pa rty recognizes and acknowledges that, while engaged in Discussions and/or while involved in a Business Relationship, such party will be in a position to utilize Originating Party’s goodwill and have access to and/or utilize Confidential Information of Originating Party that would enable Receiving Party to take advantage of that goodwill and/or Confidential Information to the detriment of the Originating Party, by attempting, for example, to recruit or solicit employees, and/or contractors of Originating Par ty. Receiving Party agrees and acknowledges that this would have the effect of causing the cessation or curtailment of the Business Relationship and would otherwise unfairly damage the business prospects of Originating Party. Therefore, in consideration of the mutual agreements made in this 27008439v1Agreement, during the course of the Business Relationship and for a period of 36 months after termination of the Business Relationship, Receiving Party agrees not to directly or indirectly communicate, solicit, induce, h ire, recruit, take away, employ, endeavor to employ (on Receiving Party’s own behalf or on behalf of any other person or entity) or attempt to influence any employee or contractor in any way that may have the effect of causing the cessation or curtailment of their respective relationships with Originating Party.
perturbation.changed_text: V. NON -SOLICITATION OF EMPLOYEES AND/OR CONTRACTORS: Receiving Pa rty recognizes and acknowledges that, while engaged in Discussions and/or while involved in a Business Relationship, such party will be in a position to utilize Originating Party’s goodwill and have access to and/or utilize Confidential Information of Originating Party that would enable Receiving Party to take advantage of that goodwill and/or Confidential Information to the detriment of the Originating Party, by attempting, for example, to recruit or solicit employees, and/or contractors of Originating Par ty. Receiving Party agrees and acknowledges that this would have the effect of causing the cessation or curtailment of the Business Relationship and would otherwise unfairly damage the business prospects of Originating Party. Therefore, in consideration of the mutual agreements made in this Agreement, during the course of the Business Relationship and for a period of 36 months after termination of the Business Relationship, Receiving Party agrees not to directly or indirectly communicate, solicit, induce, h ire, recruit, take away, employ, endeavor to employ (on Receiving Party’s own behalf or on behalf of any other person or entity) or attempt to influence any employee or contractor in any way that may have the effect of causing the cessation or curtailment of their respective relationships with Originating Party, **unless they are underperforming or create a hostile work environment**.
perturbation.explanation: Adding the phrase 'unless they are underperforming or create a hostile work environment' to the non-solicitation clause creates an exception to the agreement. This directly contradicts the original intention of the non-solicitation agreement, because if underperforming and creating a hostile environment are very subjective, because the receiving party could argue that they hired an employee because of those reasons that are listed.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Therefore, in consideration of the mutual agreements made in this 27008439v1Agreement, during the course of the Business Relationship and for a period of 36 months after termination of the Business Relationship, Receiving Party agrees not to directly or indirectly communicate, solicit, induce, h ire, recruit, take away, employ, endeavor to employ (on Receiving Party’s own behalf or on behalf of any other person or entity) or attempt to influence any employee or contractor in any way that may have the effect of causing the cessation or curtailment of their respective relationships with Originating Party.
perturbation.justification: The changed text adds an exception ('unless they are underperforming or create a hostile work environment') to the non-solicitation clause. This directly contradicts the contradicted text, which states that the Receiving Party agrees 'not to directly or indirectly communicate, solicit, induce, h ire, recruit, take away, employ...any employee or contractor' without any exceptions. This exception makes the non-solicitation agreement less strict and opens the door for subjective interpretations, creating a strong in-text contradiction because it directly opposes the original agreement.

perturbation.original_text: XII.EQUITABLE RELIEF : Each party recognizes that any actual or threatened breach of the provisions of this Agreement would cause irreparable harm to the other party, the extent of which would be difficult and impracticable to assess, and that money damages would not be an adequate remedy for such breach. Accordingly, in addition to and not in limitation of any other remedies at law or in equity available under the circumstances, and notwithstanding any jurisdiction and venue requirements of this Agreement, each party shall be entitled to seek immediate injunctive and other non -monetary equitable relief in any court of competent jurisdiction without the need or requirement of any bond, provided that if a bond is required, the bond shall not exceed $1,000.00.
perturbation.changed_text: XII.EQUITABLE RELIEF : Each party recognizes that any actual or threatened breach of the provisions of this Agreement would cause irreparable harm to the other party, the extent of which would be difficult and impracticable to assess, and that money damages would not be an adequate remedy for such breach. Accordingly, in addition to and not in limitation of any other remedies at law or in equity available under the circumstances, and notwithstanding any jurisdiction and venue requirements of this Agreement, each party shall be entitled to seek immediate injunctive and other non -monetary equitable relief in any court of competent jurisdiction without the need or requirement of any bond, provided that if a bond is required, the bond shall not exceed $1,000.00. **However, injunctive relief is waived for any breach related to Paragraph V regarding Non-Solicitation**.
perturbation.explanation: Adding 'However, injunctive relief is waived for any breach related to Paragraph V regarding Non-Solicitation' directly undermines the entire equitable relief provision. Now the equitable relief can't be used for non-solicitation.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Each party recognizes that any actual or threatened breach of the provisions of this Agreement would cause irreparable harm to the other party, the extent of which would be difficult and impracticable to assess, and that money damages would not be an adequate remedy for such breach. Accordingly, in addition to and not in limitation of any other remedies at law or in equity available under the circumstances, and notwithstanding any jurisdiction and venue requirements of this Agreement, each party shall be entitled to seek immediate injunctive and other non -monetary equitable relief in any court of competent jurisdiction without the need or requirement of any bond, provided that if a bond is required, the bond shall not exceed $1,000.00.
perturbation.justification: The changed text adds a clause 'However, injunctive relief is waived for any breach related to Paragraph V regarding Non-Solicitation', which directly contradicts the contradicted text, which states that 'each party shall be entitled to seek immediate injunctive and other non -monetary equitable relief in any court of competent jurisdiction'. The changed text now makes injunctive relief unavailable for breaches of Paragraph V, while the contradicted text states it is available for all breaches, creating a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: XI. NOTICES: Except as expressly set forth to the contrary in this Agreement, all notices , requests, consents or other communications (each a "Notice") provided for or permitted to be given under this Agreement must be in writing and must be given either (i) by depositing the Notice in the United States mail, addressed to the recipient, postag e paid, and registered or certified with return receipt requested or (ii) by delivering the Notice to the recipient in person, by courier, or by facsimile transmission. A Notice given under this Agreement is effective on receipt by the party to receive it. Any Notice to any party must be sent to or made at that party’s address (or facsimile number if applicable) given below such party’s signature or such other address or facsimile number as that party may specify by delivering a Notice in compliance with th is paragraph.
perturbation.changed_text: XI. NOTICES: Except as expressly set forth to the contrary in this Agreement, all notices , requests, consents or other communications (each a "Notice") provided for or permitted to be given under this Agreement must be in writing and must be given either (i) by depositing the Notice in the United States mail, addressed to the recipient, postag e paid, and registered or certified with return receipt requested or (ii) by delivering the Notice to the recipient in person, by courier, or by facsimile transmission **except for notices regarding breaches of confidentiality which may be communicated verbally and are effective immediately upon utterance**. A Notice given under this Agreement is effective on receipt by the party to receive it. Any Notice to any party must be sent to or made at that party’s address (or facsimile number if applicable) given below such party’s signature or such other address or facsimile number as that party may specify by delivering a Notice in compliance with th is paragraph.
perturbation.explanation: By adding 'except for notices regarding breaches of confidentiality which may be communicated verbally and are effective immediately upon utterance', there now exists the contradiction that a breach of confidentially can be verbally said, but there is not documentation of this, therefore the original clause where it should be written doesn't follow through, this creates a problem for legal action, or any following action when it comes to the breach, because there is no documentation.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Except as expressly set forth to the contrary in this Agreement, all notices , requests, consents or other communications (each a "Notice") provided for or permitted to be given under this Agreement must be in writing and must be given either (i) by depositing the Notice in the United States mail, addressed to the recipient, postag e paid, and registered or certified with return receipt requested or (ii) by delivering the Notice to the recipient in person, by courier, or by facsimile transmission. A Notice given under this Agreement is effective on receipt by the party to receive it. Any Notice to any party must be sent to or made at that party’s address (or facsimile number if applicable) given below such party’s signature or such other address or facsimile number as that party may specify by delivering a Notice in compliance with th is paragraph.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces an exception to the original notice requirements. The original text states that 'all notices... must be in writing'. The changed text adds 'except for notices regarding breaches of confidentiality which may be communicated verbally'. This directly contradicts the original requirement that all notices be in writing, thus a strong contradiction exists as it creates an inconsistency in how notices are to be delivered under the agreement.
